steps.title: Use the poor-urchin selection rate
steps.content: The study offered sunflower sea stars a nutritionally rich urchin and a nutritionally poor urchin. The sea stars selected poor urchins in 37.5 percent of trials, close to the 42.7 percent rate for rich urchins. That means they did not consistently avoid poor urchins.

steps.title: Reject unsupported comparisons
steps.content: Choice A invents other marine prey. Choice B says the sea stars are reluctant to feed on both types, which the trial rates do not show. Choice C compares sunflower sea stars with other sea stars, but no other species were tested. The result supports their potential role in controlling barrens. The answer is D.
